_comment: Multi-line stdout scenarios for the bridge-side runner. Each scenario feeds the listed lines (in order) as the agent's stdout, then asserts the runner's observable outputs: final reply, session_id, error, exit_code, and (when streaming) the list of partial chunks delivered via on_partial. Both the Python runner.run() and Node runner.run() MUST produce the listed result. Add scenarios here when a spec change touches multi-line interaction semantics (last-wins, error-mid-stream, session-with-error, etc.); both SDK test suites pick this file up automatically.

scenarios.name: session discovered at end (common CLI-wrapping case)
one-shot with session declared up front
error mid-stream (rate-limit case)
lines: AGENT_ERROR:"Upstream API rate limited. Try again in 60s."

expect.error: Upstream API rate limited. Try again in 60s.

scenarios.name: session preserved when error follows (result{is_error} shape)
streaming=false ignores partials, keeps reply body
invalid session id is ignored, no session captured
invalid session id after valid one preserves the valid
